preamble: The close front rounded vowel [y] shifts to the near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] through a process of unrounding and vowel opening. This shift occurs prominently in East High German dialects (Upper Saxon, Bavarian) as part of a broader vowel system simplification that destroyed the front rounded vowels of Middle High German. The process is distinct from but sometimes cooccurs with vowel raising in unrounded counterparts.

examples.note: Unrounding of close front rounded vowels in stressed and unstressed syllables; shows as æ-like quality in some dialects
Standard German ö (mid rounded) → Upper Saxon æ-quality variant through vowel system restructuring
Unrounding and opening of front rounded vowels in Bavarian dialects; comparable to Upper Saxon changes

period: Early Modern German onward (14th-17th century); attested in modern East High German dialects

related.label: Unrounding to close mid front (raising variant)
Vowel raising from near-open to close-mid
